<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
<title>Analytic number theory</title>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.css">
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.js"></script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/contrib/auto-render.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function(){
  function renderAll(){
    if(typeof renderMathInElement!=='function'){ return setTimeout(renderAll,50); }
    renderMathInElement(document.body,{
      delimiters:[
        {left:'$$',right:'$$',display:true},
        {left:'$',right:'$',display:false},
        {left:'\\(',right:'\\)',display:false},
        {left:'\\[',right:'\\]',display:true}
      ],
      throwOnError:false,
      macros:{
        '\\Spec':'\\Spec',
        '\\Gal':'\\Gal',
        '\\Hom':'\\Hom',
        '\\tr':'\\tr',
        '\\ad':'\\ad',
        '\\ind':'\\ind'
      }
    });
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',renderAll);
  else renderAll();
})();
</script>
<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  /* sticky sidebar TOC (wide screens only; narrow screens fall back to top-nav) */
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */
</style>

1 Prime counting 2 Explicit formula 3 Zero-free regions 4 Primes in APs 5 Sieves 6 Bombieri–Vinogradov

Analytic number theory

From counting primes to controlling them. Riemann's $\zeta(s)$, the explicit formula, zero-free regions, Dirichlet's theorem on primes in arithmetic progressions, sieve methods, and the Bombieri–Vinogradov theorem — analysis as a microscope on the integers.

blocks.html:
1. $\pi(x)$ and the prime number theorem

Let $\pi(x) = \#\{p \le x : p \text{ prime}\}$. The earliest serious prediction about $\pi(x)$ is Gauss's, made in 1792 (he was fifteen): the density of primes near $x$ should be $1/\log x$, so $$\pi(x) \;\approx\; \mathrm{Li}(x) \;=\; \int_2^x \frac{dt}{\log t}.$$ Integration by parts gives $\mathrm{Li}(x) = x/\log x + x/(\log x)^2 + 2x/(\log x)^3 + \cdots$, so the leading-order content is $\pi(x) \sim x/\log x$ — the prime number theorem (PNT) of Hadamard and de la Vallée Poussin (1896). The logarithmic integral $\mathrm{Li}(x)$ keeps the higher-order corrections and matches $\pi(x)$ to many leading digits, while $x/\log x$ undershoots by several percent in the range we can compute.

The story splits into two questions. How fast does $\pi(x)/(x/\log x) \to 1$? is a question about the error term in PNT, and the answer is intimately tied to the zeros of the Riemann zeta function. How are primes distributed within a fixed residue class $a \bmod q$? is Dirichlet's question, and its answer involves a family of $L$-functions. We treat both in this page; sieve methods (the third pillar) handle the residual cases — twin primes, almost-primes, primes in short intervals — where $\zeta$ alone is too blunt.

$\pi(x)$ vs. $x/\log x$ vs. $\mathrm{Li}(x)$
drag $x$ from $10$ to $10000$
$x = 2000$
All three curves grow like $x/\log x$ to leading order. $\mathrm{Li}(x)$ tracks $\pi(x)$ closely; $x/\log x$ undershoots by $\sim x/(\log x)^2$. PNT is the statement that the ratio $\pi(x)/(x/\log x) \to 1$, even though both curves grow without bound.

Used in
Analytic number theory · $\zeta(s)$ and the explicit formula
Analytic number theory · Dirichlet's theorem on primes in AP
Analytic number theory · Sieve methods
</section>

2. $\zeta(s)$ and the explicit formula

The Euler product $$\zeta(s) \;=\; \sum_{n \ge 1} \frac{1}{n^s} \;=\; \prod_p \frac{1}{1 - p^{-s}}, \qquad \mathrm{Re}\,s > 1$$ makes $\zeta$ an analytic encoding of the primes. Taking $-\log$ and differentiating, $$-\frac{\zeta'(s)}{\zeta(s)} \;=\; \sum_{n \ge 1} \frac{\Lambda(n)}{n^s}, \qquad \Lambda(n) = \begin{cases} \log p & n = p^k, \\ 0 & \text{else.} \end{cases}$$ The von Mangoldt $\psi(x) = \sum_{n \le x} \Lambda(n)$ is the natural prime-counting cousin: PNT is equivalent to $\psi(x) \sim x$. Apply Perron's formula to $-\zeta'/\zeta$ and shift the contour past the poles. The pole of $\zeta(s)$ at $s = 1$ becomes the residue $-(-1) \cdot x^1/1 = x$ in $\psi(x)$. Each non-trivial zero $\rho = \beta + i\gamma$ of $\zeta$ contributes a residue $-x^\rho/\rho$ via $-\zeta'(\rho)/\zeta(\rho)$'s pole. The trivial zeros at $-2, -4, \ldots$ contribute $-\tfrac12 \log(1 - x^{-2})$.

The result is Riemann's explicit formula (proved rigorously by von Mangoldt): $$\boxed{\;\psi(x) \;=\; x \;-\; \sum_{\rho} \frac{x^\rho}{\rho} \;-\; \log 2\pi \;-\; \tfrac12 \log\!\bigl(1 - x^{-2}\bigr).\;}$$ The error in $\psi(x) - x$ is exactly the sum over zeros. Riemann's hypothesis — that every non-trivial $\rho$ has $\mathrm{Re}\,\rho = 1/2$ — is precisely the assertion that $\psi(x) - x = O(x^{1/2}(\log x)^2)$, the smallest possible error.

Zeros of $\zeta(s)$ and the truncated explicit formula
add zeros, watch $\psi(x)$ converge
$N = 6$
Top: critical strip with the first $20$ non-trivial zeros (heights $\gamma_n$ from the LMFDB). Bottom: the truncated explicit formula $x - \sum_{|\gamma_k| \le \gamma_N} x^{\rho_k}/\rho_k$ vs. $\psi(x)$. Each pair of zeros adds an oscillation of frequency $\gamma_k$ and amplitude $x^{1/2}/|\rho_k|$.

See also
  • Residue theorem
  • Perron's formula

Used in
Analytic number theory · Zero-free regions and PNT proofs
</section>

3. Zero-free regions and PNT proofs

To prove $\psi(x) \sim x$, the only thing the explicit formula demands is that no zero $\rho$ has $\mathrm{Re}\,\rho = 1$. Hadamard and de la Vallée Poussin's 1896 argument is short and beautiful. From the Euler product, $$\log \zeta(\sigma + it) \;=\; \sum_p \sum_{k \ge 1} \frac{1}{k\, p^{k(\sigma + it)}} \;=\; \sum_{p,k} \frac{\cos(t k \log p) - i \sin(t k \log p)}{k\, p^{k\sigma}},$$ so $\mathrm{Re}\,\log\zeta(\sigma + it) = \sum_{p,k} \cos(tk\log p)/(k p^{k\sigma})$. The key trigonometric inequality $$3 + 4\cos\theta + \cos 2\theta \;=\; 2(1 + \cos\theta)^2 \;\ge\; 0$$ applied term-by-term to $\theta = tk\log p$ gives $$3\,\mathrm{Re}\log\zeta(\sigma) \;+\; 4\,\mathrm{Re}\log\zeta(\sigma + it) \;+\; \mathrm{Re}\log\zeta(\sigma + 2it) \;\ge\; 0,$$ i.e. $|\zeta(\sigma)^3 \zeta(\sigma+it)^4 \zeta(\sigma+2it)| \ge 1$. As $\sigma \searrow 1$, $\zeta(\sigma)$ blows up like $1/(\sigma-1)$. A double zero $\zeta(1+it) = 0$ would force the product to vanish; the inequality forbids this, so $\zeta(1+it) \ne 0$.

Sharpening the argument with growth bounds for $\zeta$ and $\zeta'/\zeta$ (Borel–Carathéodory) extends the conclusion to a thin region: $$\zeta(\sigma + it) \ne 0 \qquad \text{for} \qquad \sigma \;>\; 1 - \frac{c}{\log|t|}, \quad |t| \ge 2.$$ Combined with the explicit formula and a Hadamard product expansion, this yields PNT with effective remainder $\psi(x) = x + O(x \exp(-c\sqrt{\log x}))$. Vinogradov–Korobov sharpens this to $\sigma > 1 - c / (\log|t|)^{2/3}(\log\log|t|)^{1/3}$, the best known unconditional region.

The critical strip and the zero-free region
drag $c$, see the forbidden zone shrink
$c = 1.00$
Critical strip $0 \le \sigma \le 1$ shaded blue. Forbidden-zero region (where $\zeta \ne 0$) shaded green. The first 20 non-trivial zeros (real part $1/2$, heights $\gamma_n$) plotted as yellow dots — they all sit on the critical line, regardless of which proven region we draw.
</section>

4. Dirichlet's theorem on primes in AP

Theorem (Dirichlet, 1837). If $\gcd(a, q) = 1$, the arithmetic progression $a, a+q, a+2q, \ldots$ contains infinitely many primes.

Worked example: orthogonality of characters. The proof of Dirichlet's theorem uses character orthogonality to extract the contribution of one residue class from $\sum_{p \le x} 1$.

The proof hinges on Dirichlet $L$-functions $L(s, \chi) = \sum_n \chi(n)/n^s$, where $\chi : (\mathbb{Z}/q)^\times \to \mathbb{C}^\times$ is a multiplicative character. Orthogonality of characters gives $$\frac{1}{\varphi(q)} \sum_{\chi \bmod q} \bar\chi(a)\,\chi(n) \;=\; \mathbf{1}[n \equiv a \pmod q],$$ so summing $\log L(s, \chi)$ weighted by $\bar\chi(a)/\varphi(q)$ over all $\chi$ collapses to the partial sum $\sum_{p \equiv a} p^{-s} + O(1)$. The trivial character contributes $\log\zeta(s)$, which blows up as $s \to 1^+$. Each non-trivial $\chi$ contributes $\log L(s, \chi)$. Provided $L(1, \chi) \ne 0$ for every non-trivial $\chi$, those terms are bounded. So the prime sum diverges, and infinitely many primes hit the class $a$.

The non-vanishing $L(1, \chi) \ne 0$ is the heart of the proof. For complex $\chi$ it follows easily from the meromorphic continuation of the product $\prod_\chi L(s, \chi) = \zeta_K(s)$, the Dedekind zeta of the cyclotomic field $\mathbb{Q}(\zeta_q)$, which has a simple pole. The hard case is real (quadratic) $\chi$, where Dirichlet's class-number formula $$L(1, \chi) \;=\; \frac{2\pi h_d}{w_d \sqrt{|d|}} \quad \text{(or analogous formula for } \mathrm{Re} > 0\text{)}$$ expresses $L(1, \chi)$ as a positive multiple of the class number $h_d$ — and class numbers are positive integers.

Primes mod $q$ on a residue wheel
pick $q$, watch the equidistribution
x = 500
Each spoke is a residue class $a \bmod q$. Coprime classes (where Dirichlet predicts $\sim x/(\varphi(q)\log x)$ primes) are highlighted; non-coprime classes (where $\gcd(a, q) > 1$) catch only the prime divisor of $q$ itself, if any.

See also
  • Dirichlet characters and L-series

Used in
Analytic number theory · Bombieri–Vinogradov on average
</section>

5. Sieve methods

Erathosthenes's sieve runs as inclusion–exclusion. To count integers $n \le x$ with no prime factor $\le z$, $$S(x, z) \;=\; \sum_{n \le x}\,\sum_{d \mid \gcd(n, P(z))} \mu(d) \;=\; \sum_{d \mid P(z)} \mu(d)\, \Bigl\lfloor \frac{x}{d} \Bigr\rfloor, \qquad P(z) = \prod_{p \le z} p.$$ The naive identity is exact, but useless for primes: if $z = \sqrt{x}$, the sifted set is $\{1\} \cup \{\text{primes in } (\sqrt{x}, x]\}$, so $S(x, \sqrt{x}) = \pi(x) - \pi(\sqrt{x}) + 1$. The trouble: $P(z)$ has $2^{\pi(z)}$ divisors, each contributing an $O(1)$ rounding error, and $2^{\pi(\sqrt{x})}$ swamps the main term $x \prod_{p \le \sqrt{x}}(1 - 1/p) \sim 2 e^{-\gamma} \sqrt{x}/\log\sqrt{x}$.

Brun's idea (1915): truncate the inclusion–exclusion at $r$ levels. Brun's pure sieve uses $$\sum_{\substack{d \mid P(z) \\ \omega(d) \le 2r}} \mu(d) \mathbf{1}[d \mid n] \;\ge\; \mathbf{1}[\gcd(n, P(z)) = 1] \;\ge\; \sum_{\substack{d \mid P(z) \\ \omega(d) \le 2r+1}} \mu(d) \mathbf{1}[d \mid n].$$ Truncated upper bounds yield bounds like $\pi_2(x) = \#\{p \le x : p, p+2 \text{ both prime}\} \ll x/(\log x)^2$ — so $\sum_{p, p+2 \text{ prime}}(1/p + 1/(p+2))$ converges to Brun's constant $B_2 \approx 1.902\ldots$. The convergence is the original surprise: even if there are infinitely many twin primes, they are sparse.

Selberg (1947): replace truncation by squares. For any sequence $(\lambda_d)$ with $\lambda_1 = 1$, $$\bigl(\textstyle\sum_{d \mid n} \lambda_d\bigr)^2 \;\ge\; \mathbf{1}[\gcd(n, P(z)) = 1].$$ Summing over $n \le x$ and optimizing in $\lambda$ gives an upper bound that is best possible up to a constant — the Selberg sieve. This is the engine behind Bombieri–Vinogradov (next section), Chen's theorem (every large even number is $p + p_2$ with $p_2$ a prime or product of two primes), and Maynard–Tao bounded gaps between primes.

Truncated inclusion–exclusion vs. exact sieve
truncation level $r$, watch error oscillate
$r = 3$ z = 7
Counting integers $\le x = 200$ coprime to $P(z) = \prod_{p \le z} p$. Exact answer (green dashed) vs. truncated alternating sum (yellow). Even $r$ over-counts, odd $r$ under-counts — the inclusion–exclusion error oscillates as $r$ grows, and Brun's trick is to stop at the right truncation.

See also
  • Dirichlet series basics
</section>

6. Bombieri–Vinogradov: GRH on average

PNT for arithmetic progressions says $\pi(x; q, a) \sim \mathrm{Li}(x)/\varphi(q)$ for $\gcd(a, q) = 1$. The error term $$E(x; q, a) \;=\; \pi(x; q, a) - \frac{\mathrm{Li}(x)}{\varphi(q)}$$ is the analogue of $\psi(x) - x$ for the progression. The generalized Riemann hypothesis (GRH) for $L(s, \chi)$ would give $E(x; q, a) \ll x^{1/2} (\log x)^2$ uniformly in $q \le x$ — strong enough for almost any application. Unconditionally we have nothing like that. But on average over $q$, we essentially do.

Theorem (Bombieri 1965, A. I. Vinogradov 1965). For every $A > 0$ there exists $B > 0$ such that $$\sum_{q \le Q} \;\max_{\gcd(a, q) = 1} |E(x; q, a)| \;\ll_A\; \frac{x}{(\log x)^A}, \qquad Q = \frac{x^{1/2}}{(\log x)^B}.$$

The bound matches what GRH would give summed over $q \le \sqrt{x}$. Most arithmetic applications need exactly this $\ell^1$-on-moduli norm, not pointwise control, so the slogan "GRH on average" is fair. The proof goes through the large sieve inequality of Montgomery and Bombieri, controlling sums $\sum_{q \le Q} \sum_\chi^* |\sum_{n \le x} a_n \chi(n)|^2$ for primitive characters $\chi$; the prime-counting function feeds into this via a Vaughan-type identity that splits $\Lambda(n)$ into bilinear pieces. Pushing $Q$ past $\sqrt{x}$ is the open Elliott–Halberstam conjecture ($Q$ up to $x^{1-\varepsilon}$), which would lower the Maynard–Tao prime gap from $246$ to as small as $12$.

Distribution of $|E(x; q, a)|$ over moduli $q$
drag $x$, see the histogram
$x = 2000$
For each $q \in [2, \sqrt{x}]$ we compute $\max_{\gcd(a,q)=1} |E(x;q,a)|$ and bin the values. Bombieri–Vinogradov bounds the sum over $q$ (i.e. the bulk mass on the left of the histogram); individual $q$ may have larger error, but they are rare.

See also
  • Prototype L-functions
</section>

7. Connections

Analytic number theory translates additive / multiplicative questions about integers into questions about complex-analytic functions — and then exploits the dictionary in both directions.

Dirichlet series and L-functions

Dirichlet series are the engines: $\zeta(s)$ for primes, $L(s, \chi)$ for primes in AP, more elaborate $L$-functions for arithmetic objects. Euler products are the bridge from "sum over $n$" to "product over primes".

Modular forms and Galois representations

Hecke L-functions live both as Mellin transforms of modular forms and as compositions through Galois representations; modular forms, Hecke operators, and Galois representations form a single arc of which analytic NT is the analytic vertex.

Complex analysis

Complex analysis — residues, contour integrals, Phragmén–Lindelöf — is the proof technique. The explicit formula and zero-free regions read off the analytic structure of $\zeta$.

Sieve theory and additive combinatorics

Sieve methods (Brun, Selberg) connect to extremal combinatorics; Bombieri–Vinogradov is the strongest unconditional input for the Goldston–Pintz–Yıldırım circle, leading to bounded gaps between primes.

Open frontiers: the Riemann hypothesis (and the corresponding GRH for Dirichlet $L$-functions); the parity problem in sieve theory; Vinogradov's mean-value theorem and Bourgain–Demeter decoupling; recent work on bounded gaps between primes (Maynard, Tao).

<script>
/* =============================== shared: prime sieve + Li(x) =============================== */
const ANT = (function(){
  const N = 5200;
  const composite = new Uint8Array(N+1);
  for(let p=2; p*p<=N; p++) if(!composite[p]) for(let k=p*p; k<=N; k+=p) composite[k] = 1;
  const primes = [];
  const isPrime = new Uint8Array(N+1);
  for(let p=2; p<=N; p++) if(!composite[p]){ primes.push(p); isPrime[p] = 1; }
  // Li(x) via Simpson's rule on int_2^x dt/log t
  function Li(x){
    if(x <= 2) return 0;
    const M = 200;
    const h = (x - 2) / M;
    let s = 1/Math.log(2) + 1/Math.log(x);
    for(let k=1; k<M; k++){
      const t = 2 + k*h;
      s += (k%2 ? 4 : 2) / Math.log(t);
    }
    return s * h / 3;
  }
  function pi(x){
    let c = 0;
    for(let k=0; k<primes.length && primes[k] <= x; k++) c++;
    return c;
  }
  // first 20 imaginary parts of non-trivial zeros of zeta (Riemann zeros, LMFDB)
  const ZEROS = [
    14.134725142, 21.022039639, 25.010857580, 30.424876126, 32.935061588,
    37.586178159, 40.918719012, 43.327073281, 48.005150881, 49.773832478,
    52.970321478, 56.446247697, 59.347044003, 60.831778525, 65.112544048,
    67.079810529, 69.546401711, 72.067157674, 75.704690699, 77.144840069
  ];
  return {primes, isPrime, Li, pi, ZEROS, N};
})();
</script>

<script>
/* =============================== SECTION 1 widget: pi(x) vs x/log x vs Li(x) =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg = document.querySelector("#pnt-svg");
  const xR = document.querySelector("#pnt-x");
  const xv = document.querySelector("#pnt-xv");
  const out = document.querySelector("#pnt-out");
  const W = 620, H = 320, pad = 50;

  function draw(){
    svg.innerHTML = "";
    const X = parseInt(xR.value, 10);
    xv.textContent = `x = ${X}`;
    const samples = 80;
    const xs = [], piX = [], xlogX = [], LiX = [];
    let pCount = 0;
    let pIdx = 0;
    for(let i=1; i<=samples; i++){
      const x = Math.round(X * i / samples);
      while(pIdx < ANT.primes.length && ANT.primes[pIdx] <= x){ pCount++; pIdx++; }
      xs.push(x);
      piX.push(pCount);
      xlogX.push(x < 3 ? 0 : x / Math.log(x));
      LiX.push(ANT.Li(x));
    }
    const ymax = Math.max(...piX, ...LiX) * 1.1 + 1;
    const xmin = 2;
    const toX = x => pad + (x-xmin)/(X-xmin)*(W-2*pad);
    const toY = y => H - pad - y/ymax*(H-2*pad);

    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:pad,y1:toY(0),x2:W-pad,y2:toY(0),stroke:"var(--mute)"}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:pad,y1:toY(0),x2:pad,y2:toY(ymax),stroke:"var(--mute)"}));

    // x-axis ticks
    for(let k=0; k<=5; k++){
      const v = Math.round(X*k/5);
      const t = SVG("text",{x:toX(Math.max(xmin,v)), y:toY(0)+18, "text-anchor":"middle", "font-size":11, fill:"var(--mute)"});
      t.textContent = v; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // y-axis ticks
    for(let k=0; k<=4; k++){
      const v = Math.round(ymax*k/4);
      const t = SVG("text",{x:pad-8, y:toY(v)+4, "text-anchor":"end", "font-size":11, fill:"var(--mute)"});
      t.textContent = v; svg.appendChild(t);
    }

    function path(values, color, dash){
      let d = "";
      for(let i=0; i<xs.length; i++){
        d += (i===0?"M":"L") + toX(xs[i]) + " " + toY(values[i]) + " ";
      }
      const p = SVG("path",{d, fill:"none", stroke:color, "stroke-width":2});
      if(dash) p.setAttribute("stroke-dasharray", dash);
      svg.appendChild(p);
    }
    path(xlogX, "var(--pink)", "5 4");
    path(LiX, "var(--green)", "4 3");
    path(piX, "var(--yellow)", null);

    // legend (viewport coords)
    const items = [
      ["— π(x) (exact)", "var(--yellow)"],
      ["- - Li(x)", "var(--green)"],
      ["- - x / log x", "var(--pink)"]
    ];
    items.forEach((it, i) => {
      const t = SVG("text",{x:pad+10, y:pad+14+i*16, "font-size":12, fill:it[1]});
      t.textContent = it[0]; svg.appendChild(t);
    });

    const piVal = piX[piX.length-1];
    const xLog = xlogX[xlogX.length-1];
    const li = LiX[LiX.length-1];
    out.textContent = `at x = ${X}:  π(x) = ${piVal},  x/log x ≈ ${xLog.toFixed(1)},  Li(x) ≈ ${li.toFixed(1)}.   ratio π(x)/(x/log x) = ${(piVal/xLog).toFixed(4)}`;
  }
  xR.addEventListener("input", draw);
  draw();
})();
</script>

(function(){

  const NR = document.querySelector("#ex-N");
  const Nv = document.querySelector("#ex-Nv");
  const out = document.querySelector("#ex-out");

    // ===== top: critical strip + zeros =====

    // axes

      t.textContent = `σ=${s}`; svg.appendChild(t);

      const active = k < N;
      const color = active ? "var(--yellow)" : "var(--mute)";
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:sx(0.5), cy:sy(g), r:active?4:2.5, fill:color}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:sx(0.5), cy:sy(-g), r:active?4:2.5, fill:color}));
    }
    const tt = SVG("text",{x:pad, y:stripY0-8, "font-size":12, fill:"var(--ink)"});
    tt.textContent = "critical strip 0 ≤ σ ≤ 1, zeros at σ = 1/2 (yellow = active)"; svg.appendChild(tt);

    // ===== bottom: psi(x) vs truncated explicit formula =====
    const plotY0 = stripY0 + stripH + gap + 10;
    const xmin = 2, xmax = 100;
    const px = x => pad + (x - xmin)/(xmax - xmin)*(W - 2*pad);

    // exact psi(x)
    const xs = [], psi = [];
    let acc = 0;
    let nextSample = 1;
    const Mx = 200;
    for(let m=1; m<=Mx; m++){
      const x = xmin + (xmax-xmin)*m/Mx;
      acc = 0;
      for(let n=2; n<=Math.floor(x); n++){
        // count Lambda(n): log p if n=p^k else 0
        let r = n;
        let p = 0;
        for(let q=2; q*q<=r; q++){
          if(r%q===0){ p = q; while(r%q===0) r /= q; break; }
        }
        if(r > 1){ if(p===0 || r===n) p = r; else { p = -1; } }
        if(p > 0){
          // verify n is a prime power
          let nn = n;
          while(nn % p === 0) nn /= p;
          if(nn === 1) acc += Math.log(p);
        }
      }
      xs.push(x); psi.push(acc);
      nextSample++;
    }
    // truncated explicit formula
    const approx = xs.map(x => {
      let val = x;
      for(let k=0; k<N && k<ANT.ZEROS.length; k++){
        const g = ANT.ZEROS[k];
        // rho = 1/2 + i g, x^rho/rho + x^{bar rho}/bar rho = 2 Re(x^rho / rho)
        // x^rho = x^{1/2} * (cos(g log x) + i sin(g log x))
        const lx = Math.log(x);
        const xr = Math.sqrt(x) * Math.cos(g*lx);
        const xi = Math.sqrt(x) * Math.sin(g*lx);
        // 1/rho = (1/2 - i g)/(1/4 + g^2)
        const denom = 0.25 + g*g;
        const rRe = 0.5/denom, rIm = -g/denom;
        // x^rho / rho real part:
        const re = xr*rRe - xi*rIm;
        val -= 2*re;
      }
      return val;
    });

    const ymin = 0, ymax = xmax * 1.15;
    const py = y => plotY0 + plotH - (y - ymin)/(ymax - ymin)*plotH;

    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:px(xmin), y1:py(0), x2:px(xmax), y2:py(0), stroke:"var(--mute)"}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:px(xmin), y1:py(0), x2:px(xmin), y2:py(ymax), stroke:"var(--mute)"}));
    // y = x reference
    let dRef = "";
    for(let i=0; i<xs.length; i++){
      dRef += (i===0?"M":"L") + px(xs[i]) + " " + py(xs[i]) + " ";
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:dRef, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--mute)", "stroke-width":1, "stroke-dasharray":"3 4"}));
    // exact psi
    let dPsi = "";
    for(let i=0; i<xs.length; i++){
      dPsi += (i===0?"M":"L") + px(xs[i]) + " " + py(psi[i]) + " ";
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:dPsi, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--green)", "stroke-width":2}));
    // approximation
    let dApp = "";
    for(let i=0; i<xs.length; i++){
      const v = Math.max(ymin, Math.min(ymax, approx[i]));
      dApp += (i===0?"M":"L") + px(xs[i]) + " " + py(v) + " ";
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:dApp, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--yellow)", "stroke-width":2}));
    // ticks
    for(let k=0; k<=5; k++){
      const v = Math.round(xmin + (xmax-xmin)*k/5);
      const t = SVG("text",{x:px(v), y:py(0)+15, "text-anchor":"middle", "font-size":11, fill:"var(--mute)"});
      t.textContent = v; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // legend
    const li = [
      ["— ψ(x) (exact)", "var(--green)"],
      [`— x − Σ x^ρ/ρ over ${N} zero pairs`, "var(--yellow)"],
      ["- - y = x (main term)", "var(--mute)"]
    ];
    li.forEach((it, i) => {
      const t = SVG("text",{x:pad+10, y:plotY0+14+i*15, "font-size":11, fill:it[1]});
      t.textContent = it[0]; svg.appendChild(t);
    });

    out.textContent = `${N} pairs of zeros active. At x = 50: ψ(x) = ${psi[Math.floor(Mx*0.49)].toFixed(2)}, truncated approximation = ${approx[Math.floor(Mx*0.49)].toFixed(2)}.`;
  }
  NR.addEventListener("input", draw);
  draw();
})();
</script>

<script>
/* =============================== SECTION 3 widget: critical strip + zero-free region =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg = document.querySelector("#zf-svg");
  const cR = document.querySelector("#zf-c");
  const cv = document.querySelector("#zf-cv");
  const mode = document.querySelector("#zf-mode");
  const W = 620, H = 360, pad = 50;
  const sigmin = -0.2, sigmax = 1.4;
  const tmax = 60;
  const sx = s => pad + (s - sigmin)/(sigmax - sigmin)*(W - 2*pad);
  const sy = t => H/2 - t/tmax * (H/2 - pad);

  function draw(){
    svg.innerHTML = "";
    const c = parseFloat(cR.value);
    cv.textContent = `c = ${c.toFixed(2)}`;
    const m = mode.value;

    // critical-strip shading 0..1
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:sx(0), y:pad, width:sx(1)-sx(0), height:H-2*pad,
      fill:"rgba(88,196,221,0.10)"}));

    // forbidden-zero region: shade where |t| > something, sigma > boundary(t)
    // build a polygon (left edge = sigma_*(t), right edge = sigmax)
    function boundary(t){
      const at = Math.max(2, Math.abs(t));
      if(m === "rh") return 0.5;
      if(m === "vk") return 1 - c / Math.pow(Math.log(at), 2/3);
      return 1 - c/Math.log(at);
    }
    const Nt = 100;
    let dpath = "";
    // upper edge
    for(let i=0; i<=Nt; i++){
      const t = -tmax + 2*tmax*i/Nt;
      const sig = boundary(t);
      const x = sx(Math.max(sigmin, Math.min(sigmax, sig)));
      const y = sy(t);
      dpath += (i===0?"M":"L") + x + " " + y + " ";
    }
    // close along right edge
    dpath += "L" + sx(sigmax) + " " + sy(tmax) + " ";
    dpath += "L" + sx(sigmax) + " " + sy(-tmax) + " Z";
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:dpath, fill:"rgba(131,193,103,0.15)", stroke:"var(--green)", "stroke-width":1.2}));

    // axes: real/imaginary
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:sx(sigmin), y1:sy(0), x2:sx(sigmax), y2:sy(0), stroke:"var(--mute)"}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:sx(0), y1:pad, x2:sx(0), y2:H-pad, stroke:"var(--mute)"}));
    // critical line σ = 1/2
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:sx(0.5), y1:pad, x2:sx(0.5), y2:H-pad,
      stroke:"var(--cyan)", "stroke-width":1.2, "stroke-dasharray":"4 4"}));
    // line σ = 1
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:sx(1), y1:pad, x2:sx(1), y2:H-pad,
      stroke:"var(--mute)", "stroke-width":1.2, "stroke-dasharray":"3 5"}));
    // pole at s = 1
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:sx(1), cy:sy(0), r:5, fill:"var(--pink)"}));

    // ticks
    [0, 0.5, 1].forEach(s => {
      const t = SVG("text",{x:sx(s), y:H-pad+18, "text-anchor":"middle", "font-size":11, fill:"var(--mute)"});
      t.textContent = `σ=${s}`; svg.appendChild(t);
    });
    [-50, -25, 25, 50].forEach(t => {
      const tt = SVG("text",{x:sx(0)+6, y:sy(t)+4, "font-size":11, fill:"var(--mute)"});
      tt.textContent = `t=${t}`; svg.appendChild(tt);
    });

    // zeros
    for(let k=0; k<ANT.ZEROS.length; k++){
      const g = ANT.ZEROS[k];
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:sx(0.5), cy:sy(g), r:3.5, fill:"var(--yellow)"}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:sx(0.5), cy:sy(-g), r:3.5, fill:"var(--yellow)"}));
    }

    // legend
    const li = [
      ["█ critical strip 0 ≤ σ ≤ 1", "var(--cyan)"],
      ["█ proven zero-free region", "var(--green)"],
      ["• non-trivial zeros (σ = 1/2)", "var(--yellow)"]
    ];
    li.forEach((it, i) => {
      const t = SVG("text",{x:pad+8, y:pad+14+i*16, "font-size":12, fill:it[1]});
      t.textContent = it[0]; svg.appendChild(t);
    });
  }
  cR.addEventListener("input", draw);
  mode.addEventListener("change", draw);
  draw();
})();
</script>

<script>
/* =============================== SECTION 4 widget: residue wheel =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg = document.querySelector("#dr-svg");
  const qS = document.querySelector("#dr-q");
  const xR = document.querySelector("#dr-x");
  const xv = document.querySelector("#dr-xv");
  const out = document.querySelector("#dr-out");
  const W = 620, H = 360;
  const cx = W/2, cy = H/2;
  const R = 130;

  function gcd(a,b){ while(b){ [a,b] = [b, a%b]; } return a; }
  function phi(n){
    let c = 0;
    for(let k=1; k<=n; k++) if(gcd(k,n)===1) c++;
    return c;
  }

  function draw(){
    svg.innerHTML = "";
    const q = parseInt(qS.value, 10);
    const X = parseInt(xR.value, 10);
    xv.textContent = `x = ${X}`;

    // count primes by class
    const counts = new Array(q).fill(0);
    for(let i=0; i<ANT.primes.length; i++){
      const p = ANT.primes[i];
      if(p > X) break;
      counts[p % q]++;
    }
    const totalCop = counts.reduce((a,c,i) => a + (gcd(i,q)===1 ? c : 0), 0);
    const f = phi(q);
    const expected = totalCop / f;
    const maxC = Math.max(...counts, 1);
    const Rmax = R + 60;

    // wheel center marker
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx, cy, r:R, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--line)", "stroke-width":1.2}));

    // bars
    for(let a=0; a<q; a++){
      const ang = -Math.PI/2 + 2*Math.PI*a/q;
      const cop = (gcd(a,q)===1);
      const c = counts[a];
      const len = (c/maxC) * 70;
      const x1 = cx + Math.cos(ang)*R;
      const y1 = cy + Math.sin(ang)*R;
      const x2 = cx + Math.cos(ang)*(R+len);
      const y2 = cy + Math.sin(ang)*(R+len);
      const color = cop ? "var(--yellow)" : "var(--mute)";
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1,y1,x2,y2,stroke:color,"stroke-width":cop?5:2.5}));
      // label
      const xl = cx + Math.cos(ang)*(Rmax+8);
      const yl = cy + Math.sin(ang)*(Rmax+8) + 4;
      const t = SVG("text",{x:xl, y:yl, "text-anchor":"middle", "font-size":12,
        fill: cop ? "var(--yellow)" : "var(--mute)",
        "font-weight": cop ? 600 : 400});
      t.textContent = `${a}: ${c}`;
      svg.appendChild(t);
    }

    // central caption
    const t1 = SVG("text",{x:cx, y:cy-10, "text-anchor":"middle", "font-size":14, fill:"var(--ink)", "font-weight":600});
    t1.textContent = `q = ${q}`; svg.appendChild(t1);
    const t2 = SVG("text",{x:cx, y:cy+10, "text-anchor":"middle", "font-size":12, fill:"var(--mute)"});
    t2.textContent = `φ(q) = ${f}`; svg.appendChild(t2);
    const t3 = SVG("text",{x:cx, y:cy+30, "text-anchor":"middle", "font-size":11, fill:"var(--cyan)"});
    t3.textContent = `expected per coprime class ≈ ${expected.toFixed(1)}`; svg.appendChild(t3);

    // readout
    const lines = [];
    for(let a=0; a<q; a++){
      if(gcd(a,q)===1){
        lines.push(`π(x; ${q}, ${a}) = ${counts[a]}`);
      }
    }
    out.textContent = lines.join("   ") + `   |   total in coprime classes = ${totalCop}, perfectly equidistributed would give ${expected.toFixed(2)} per class.`;
  }
  qS.addEventListener("change", draw);
  xR.addEventListener("input", draw);
  draw();
})();
</script>

<script>
/* =============================== SECTION 5 widget: truncated inclusion-exclusion =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg = document.querySelector("#sv-svg");
  const rR = document.querySelector("#sv-r");
  const rv = document.querySelector("#sv-rv");
  const zR = document.querySelector("#sv-z");
  const zv = document.querySelector("#sv-zv");
  const out = document.querySelector("#sv-out");
  const W = 620, H = 320, pad = 50;
  const X = 200;

  function smallPrimes(z){
    const ps = [];
    for(let p=2; p<=z; p++){
      let prime = true;
      for(let q=2; q*q<=p; q++) if(p%q===0){ prime = false; break; }
      if(prime) ps.push(p);
    }
    return ps;
  }
  function squarefreeDivisors(ps){
    const out = [{d:1, omega:0, mu:1}];
    for(const p of ps){
      const cur = out.slice();
      for(const e of cur){
        out.push({d:e.d*p, omega:e.omega+1, mu:-e.mu});
      }
    }
    return out;
  }

  function compute(z, rmax){
    const ps = smallPrimes(z);
    const divs = squarefreeDivisors(ps);
    // exact: sum mu(d) floor(X/d)
    let exact = 0;
    for(const e of divs) exact += e.mu * Math.floor(X/e.d);
    // truncated up to omega <= r, for r = 0..rmax+1
    const trunc = [];
    for(let r=0; r<=rmax; r++){
      let s = 0;
      for(const e of divs){ if(e.omega <= r) s += e.mu * Math.floor(X/e.d); }
      trunc.push(s);
    }
    // Mertens-style main term: X * prod_{p<=z} (1 - 1/p)
    let main = X;
    for(const p of ps) main *= (1 - 1/p);
    return {exact, trunc, main, ps};
  }

  function draw(){
    svg.innerHTML = "";
    const r = parseInt(rR.value, 10);
    const z = parseInt(zR.value, 10);
    rv.textContent = `r = ${r}`;
    zv.textContent = `z = ${z}`;

    const rmaxKnob = parseInt(rR.max, 10);
    const data = compute(z, rmaxKnob);
    const numPrimes = data.ps.length;
    const rmaxPlot = Math.min(rmaxKnob, numPrimes);

    const xmin = 0, xmax = rmaxPlot;
    const allVals = data.trunc.slice(0, rmaxPlot+1).concat([data.exact, data.main]);
    let ymin = Math.min(...allVals), ymax = Math.max(...allVals);
    const margin = (ymax - ymin) * 0.15 + 1;
    ymin -= margin; ymax += margin;

    const toX = x => pad + (x - xmin)/(xmax - xmin)*(W - 2*pad);
    const toY = y => H - pad - (y - ymin)/(ymax - ymin)*(H - 2*pad);

    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:pad, y1:H-pad, x2:W-pad, y2:H-pad, stroke:"var(--mute)"}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:pad, y1:pad, x2:pad, y2:H-pad, stroke:"var(--mute)"}));

    // exact line (green dashed)
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:pad, y1:toY(data.exact), x2:W-pad, y2:toY(data.exact),
      stroke:"var(--green)", "stroke-width":1.5, "stroke-dasharray":"5 4"}));
    // main term line (cyan dashed)
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:pad, y1:toY(data.main), x2:W-pad, y2:toY(data.main),
      stroke:"var(--cyan)", "stroke-width":1.5, "stroke-dasharray":"3 4"}));

    // truncated values as connected dots
    let d = "";
    for(let i=0; i<=rmaxPlot; i++){
      const px = toX(i);
      const py = toY(data.trunc[i]);
      d += (i===0?"M":"L") + px + " " + py + " ";
      const active = (i === r);
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:px, cy:py, r:active?6:3.5,
        fill: active ? "var(--yellow)" : "var(--pink)"}));
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--pink)", "stroke-width":1.5}));

    // x ticks
    for(let i=0; i<=rmaxPlot; i++){
      const t = SVG("text",{x:toX(i), y:H-pad+16, "text-anchor":"middle", "font-size":11, fill:"var(--mute)"});
      t.textContent = i; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // y ticks
    for(let k=0; k<=4; k++){
      const v = Math.round(ymin + (ymax-ymin)*k/4);
      const t = SVG("text",{x:pad-8, y:toY(v)+4, "text-anchor":"end", "font-size":11, fill:"var(--mute)"});
      t.textContent = v; svg.appendChild(t);
    }

    // legend
    const li = [
      ["• truncated alternating sum (up to r terms)", "var(--pink)"],
      [`- - exact ${"S(X,z)"} = ${data.exact}`, "var(--green)"],
      [`- - Mertens main term X·Π(1−1/p) ≈ ${data.main.toFixed(2)}`, "var(--cyan)"]
    ];
    li.forEach((it, i) => {
      const t = SVG("text",{x:pad+8, y:pad+14+i*15, "font-size":11, fill:it[1]});
      t.textContent = it[0]; svg.appendChild(t);
    });

    out.textContent = `X = 200, primes ≤ z = [${data.ps.join(", ")}].   truncated at r = ${r}: ${data.trunc[Math.min(r, rmaxPlot)]}.   exact: ${data.exact}.   error: ${(data.trunc[Math.min(r, rmaxPlot)] - data.exact).toFixed(0)}`;
  }
  rR.addEventListener("input", draw);
  zR.addEventListener("input", draw);
  draw();
})();
</script>

<script>
/* =============================== SECTION 6 widget: Bombieri-Vinogradov error histogram =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg = document.querySelector("#bv-svg");
  const xR = document.querySelector("#bv-x");
  const xv = document.querySelector("#bv-xv");
  const out = document.querySelector("#bv-out");
  const W = 620, H = 320, pad = 50;

  function gcd(a,b){ while(b){ [a,b] = [b, a%b]; } return a; }
  function phi(n){
    let c = 0;
    for(let k=1; k<=n; k++) if(gcd(k,n)===1) c++;
    return c;
  }

  function draw(){
    svg.innerHTML = "";
    const X = parseInt(xR.value, 10);
    xv.textContent = `x = ${X}`;
    const Q = Math.floor(Math.sqrt(X));
    const Lix = ANT.Li(X);

    // for each q in [3, Q], compute max_a |pi(x;q,a) - Li(x)/phi(q)|
    const errs = [];
    for(let q=3; q<=Q; q++){
      const cnt = new Array(q).fill(0);
      for(let i=0; i<ANT.primes.length; i++){
        const p = ANT.primes[i];
        if(p > X) break;
        cnt[p % q]++;
      }
      const fq = phi(q);
      const expected = Lix / fq;
      let maxErr = 0;
      for(let a=0; a<q; a++){
        if(gcd(a,q) !== 1) continue;
        const e = Math.abs(cnt[a] - expected);
        if(e > maxErr) maxErr = e;
      }
      errs.push(maxErr);
    }

    if(errs.length === 0){
      out.textContent = "x too small — pick larger x.";
      return;
    }

    // histogram
    const eMax = Math.max(...errs);
    const bins = 14;
    const counts = new Array(bins).fill(0);
    for(const e of errs){
      const b = Math.min(bins-1, Math.floor(e/(eMax+1e-9) * bins));
      counts[b]++;
    }
    const cMax = Math.max(...counts);
    const xmin = 0, xmax = bins;
    const ymin = 0, ymax = cMax * 1.15 + 1;
    const toX = i => pad + i/bins*(W-2*pad);
    const toY = y => H - pad - y/ymax*(H-2*pad);

    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:pad, y1:toY(0), x2:W-pad, y2:toY(0), stroke:"var(--mute)"}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:pad, y1:toY(0), x2:pad, y2:toY(ymax), stroke:"var(--mute)"}));

    // bars
    const bw = (W - 2*pad)/bins - 2;
    for(let i=0; i<bins; i++){
      const h = toY(0) - toY(counts[i]);
      svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:toX(i)+1, y:toY(counts[i]), width:bw, height:h,
        fill:"var(--violet)", opacity:0.85, stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    }

    // average and total error
    let totErr = 0; for(const e of errs) totErr += e;
    const avg = totErr / errs.length;

    // label x-axis (error magnitude)
    for(let k=0; k<=bins; k+=Math.max(1,Math.floor(bins/7))){
      const v = (k/bins * eMax).toFixed(1);
      const t = SVG("text",{x:toX(k), y:toY(0)+16, "text-anchor":"middle", "font-size":10, fill:"var(--mute)"});
      t.textContent = v; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    const xt = SVG("text",{x:W/2, y:H-8, "text-anchor":"middle", "font-size":11, fill:"var(--mute)"});
    xt.textContent = `bin: max_a |π(x;q,a) − Li(x)/φ(q)|`;
    svg.appendChild(xt);

    // y label
    for(let k=0; k<=4; k++){
      const v = Math.round(ymax*k/4);
      const t = SVG("text",{x:pad-8, y:toY(v)+4, "text-anchor":"end", "font-size":11, fill:"var(--mute)"});
      t.textContent = v; svg.appendChild(t);
    }

    // legend
    const sumLabel = SVG("text",{x:pad+8, y:pad+14, "font-size":12, fill:"var(--cyan)"});
    sumLabel.textContent = `Σ_q max|E| = ${totErr.toFixed(1)}    avg = ${avg.toFixed(2)}    Q = √x = ${Q}`;
    svg.appendChild(sumLabel);
    const bvLabel = SVG("text",{x:pad+8, y:pad+30, "font-size":12, fill:"var(--mute)"});
    bvLabel.textContent = `BV bound x/(log x)^A: at x = ${X}, x/(log x)^3 ≈ ${(X/Math.pow(Math.log(X),3)).toFixed(1)}`;
    svg.appendChild(bvLabel);

    out.textContent = `${errs.length} moduli q ∈ [3, ${Q}].  Σ_q max_a |E(x;q,a)| = ${totErr.toFixed(1)};  bulk of mass sits at small errors — that is BV's content.`;
  }
  xR.addEventListener("input", draw);
  draw();
})();
</script>

<script>
/* sidebar TOC builder + active-section highlighting */
(function(){
  function build(){
    const aside = document.querySelector('aside.sidetoc');
    const topnav = document.querySelector('nav.toc');
    if(!aside || !topnav) return;
    const srcs = [...topnav.querySelectorAll('a[href^="#"]')];
    if(!srcs.length) return;
    const title = document.createElement('div');
    title.className = 'sidetoc-title';
    title.textContent = 'On this page';
    aside.appendChild(title);
    const ol = document.createElement('ol');
    srcs.forEach(src => {
      const li = document.createElement('li');
      const a = document.createElement('a');
      a.href = src.getAttribute('href');
      a.textContent = src.textContent.replace(/\u00a0/g,' ').trim();
      li.appendChild(a); ol.appendChild(li);
    });
    aside.appendChild(ol);
    const links = [...aside.querySelectorAll('a')];
    const sections = [];
    links.forEach(a => {
      const id = a.getAttribute('href').slice(1);
      const s = document.getElementById(id);
      if(s) sections.push({a, s});
    });
    if(!sections.length) return;
    function update(){
      const threshold = 140;
      let activeLink = sections[0].a;
      for(const {a,s} of sections){
        if(s.getBoundingClientRect().top <= threshold) activeLink = a;
      }
      links.forEach(x => x.classList.remove('active'));
      activeLink.classList.add('active');
    }
    update();
    window.addEventListener('scroll', update, {passive:true});
    window.addEventListener('resize', update);
  }
  if(document.readyState === 'loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', build);
  else build();
})();
</script>
<script>
(function(){
  function start(){ if(window.MVQuiz) MVQuiz.init('analytic-number-theory'); }
  if (document.readyState === 'loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', start);
  else start();
})();
</script>
